page: Microsoft Azure E4s_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Esv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes an L3 cache of 480 MB. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Benchmark data reveals a mixture of single-core strength and multi-core limitations. Single-threaded gzip compression is strong at 32.55 GB/sec, and single-core stress-ng CPU performance is average at 2034.29. However, multi-core performance is weak, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 4104.86 and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scoring 26.16 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is average for large-block uncached reads at 47.67 GB/sec, but weak for cached small-block reads at 304.9 GB/sec and large-block uncached writes at 22.69 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density of 8.0 GB per vCPU provides a favorable resource profile for memory-bound workloads without the expense of scaling up vCPU counts. However, the weak multi-core scaling and lack of local storage present clear tradeoffs for compute-heavy or high-I/O local database tasks. This instance is best suited for memory-optimized workloads that do not require high parallel processing power, such as medium-sized caching layers, single-threaded data processing, and lightweight database hosting utilizing remote Premium Storage.
description: Microsoft Azure E4s_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance from the Esv7 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs from 2 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores clocked at 4.2 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks show strong single-threaded performance, with gzip compression reaching 32.55 GB/sec, though multi-core performance and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak. This resource profile is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive applications that do not demand heavy parallel processing. It is suited for memory-optimized workloads, caching layers, and database hosting with remote storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4s_v7 is an Esv7-family memory-optimized server with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and Premium Storage support, showing strong single-threaded performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4s_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and strong single-threaded benchmark performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated high-frequency Intel Xeon processors and a high memory-to-core ratio for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor clocked at 4.2 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong single-threaded performance with gzip compression reaching 32.55 GB/sec
No local storage, utilizing remote Premium Storage options instead
Best suited for memory-intensive workloads, caching layers, and lightweight databases
